title: lagerstroemia subangulata
intro: Lagerstroemia subangulata, also known as the Southern swamp crape myrtle, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lythraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Lagerstroemia subangulata include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and improve digestion, making it a valuable plant for promoting overall well-being. Lagerstroemia subangulata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, which can be consumed as a tea or used topically to apply its medicinal properties.
